paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. V. Excluding a tree of radius two and a complete multipartite graph
summary: Conjecture 1.3 — polynomial chi-boundedness for every forest-free graph class — remains open in full generality as of May 2026. Significant partial progress has been made: Nguyen (arXiv:2512.24907, 2025) proved the conjecture for H = P_5, the five-vertex path, resolving a case open since 1985; Davies and Yuditsky (arXiv:2407.16882, 2024) proved it for all forests H when the host graphs have bounded boxicity. The ongoing Scott–Seymour series establishes polynomial bounds for several additional tree families, but no uniform proof for all forests exists.

since_posted.title: Polynomial χ-boundedness for excluding P₅
since_posted.claim: Proves that every P₅-free graph G satisfies χ(G) ≤ f(ω(G)) for a polynomial f, establishing Conjecture 1.3 for the specific forest H = P₅ (the five-vertex path).

since_posted.title: Polynomial Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture for graphs of bounded boxicity
since_posted.claim: Proves Conjecture 1.3 for every forest H when restricted to intersection graphs of axis-aligned boxes in ℝ^d (graphs of bounded boxicity).

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper proves near-linear stable sets (size |G|^{1-o(1)}) for {H, K_{k+1}}-free graphs when H is a forest, and linear stable sets for multibroom-free graphs; deduces a fractional-colouring version of the polynomial chi-boundedness conjecture for multibrooms. Does not establish polynomial chi(G) <= f(omega(G)) for ordinary chromatic number in general.
Paper concerns stable sets in K_t-subdivision-free graphs (polylogarithmic bound), not forest-free graphs; has at most indirect relevance to Conjecture 1.3.
Paper disproves the weak coarse Menger conjecture; no apparent connection to Conjecture 1.3 about polynomial chi-boundedness for forest-free graphs.

search_queries: polynomial chi-bounded forest-free graphs conjecture Scott Seymour 2202.05557
Polynomial chi-boundedness excluding P5 Nguyen 2025 arxiv
Polynomial Gyarfas-Sumner conjecture bounded boxicity Davies Yuditsky 2024
notes: Conjecture 1.3 is closely related to Esperet's conjecture (polynomial chi-boundedness for hereditary classes, now disproved in general) and to the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture (which concerns bounded chromatic number, not polynomial bounds). The P5 case (arXiv:2512.24907) and the bounded-boxicity case (arXiv:2407.16882) are the most notable post-2023 advances. Internal reference 2508.14332 appears unrelated.
